name: Zhao Gongming
description: Zhao Gongming, also known as Zhao Xuantan, is a prominent figure in Chinese folk religion and mythology, often revered as a wealth god and a powerful protector. Originally a human during the late Shang Dynasty, he was a hermit and a skilled warrior. He gained prominence through his role in the classic novel *Investiture of the Gods* (Fengshen Yanyi), where he initially fought against the Zhou Dynasty forces. During the battles, he displayed formidable magical abilities, wielding powerful artifacts and commanding supernatural forces. Ultimately, he was killed in battle by a curse. After his death, he was appointed as the Black Tiger Altar Marshal, tasked with managing plagues and disasters. Over time, his role evolved, and he became widely venerated as a wealth god, particularly by merchants and businesses seeking prosperity and protection from financial misfortune. He is often depicted riding a black tiger, holding a golden rod, and surrounded by treasures. His stern visage and powerful aura inspire both respect and a sense of security. While sometimes associated with martial prowess, his primary function in modern worship is as a bringer of wealth and good fortune, ensuring success in commercial endeavors and safeguarding against financial ruin. He is considered one of the Five Wealth Gods (Wu Cai Shen) and is frequently invoked during business openings, festivals, and other auspicious occasions. His influence extends across various regions of China and among Chinese communities worldwide, solidifying his position as a significant deity in the Chinese pantheon.
subtitle: Wealth God and Protector Deity

keyMyths.title: Investiture of the Gods
keyMyths.description: Zhao Gongming appears in Fengshen Yanyi as a powerful warrior fighting for the Shang Dynasty. He is eventually defeated and killed by Jiang Ziya's forces.

keyMyths.title: Appointment as Black Tiger Altar Marshal
keyMyths.description: After his death, Zhao Gongming is appointed as the Black Tiger Altar Marshal. This role involves managing plagues and disasters, showcasing his power.

keyMyths.title: Evolution into a Wealth God
keyMyths.description: Over time, Zhao Gongming's role evolved from a marshal to a widely venerated wealth god. He is now primarily associated with bringing wealth and prosperity.

sources.author: Xu Zhonglin
sources.description: A classic Ming dynasty novel that features Zhao Gongming as a key character.
sources.text: A classic Ming dynasty novel that features Zhao Gongming as a key character.
